title: Visionblox Interplanetary Spacesuit Benchmark, Phase-3-hardened
attribution: A. Khaalis Wooden, Sr., MBA; MSIT Candidate, Southern New Hampshire University / Visionblox LLC / Zuup Innovation Lab
framework: ZCS-6 Phase 2 artifact, v1.1 = post-Phase-3-attack hardened
epistemic_markers_required: Every assertion in any compliant solution carries VERIFIED / PLAUSIBLE / SPECULATIVE marker per ZCS-6 epistemic discipline.
problem_statement: Specify falsifiable success criteria for an interplanetary-class spacesuit system, where the unsolved engineering surface is autonomous compliance-graded life-support decision-making under deep-space communication latency, surviving multi-year deployment without resupply, with cryptographically auditable provenance for medical, regulatory, and liability oversight.

scope.reference_architecture: NASA Design Reference Architecture 5.0 (Mars surface campaign) or equivalent published reference

substrate_scope.description: Floors evaluable on commodity hardware (Jetson AGX Orin / Orin Nano + Linux + open-source dependencies). Verification protocols specified to third-party-executable level. These floors govern Phase 5 working-code milestone.

shell_scope.description: Floors requiring industrial-scale test facilities (vacuum chambers, JSC-Mars-1A regolith chambers, parabolic flight, lunar analog facilities, particle-beam accelerators). Specified to procurement-package level — sufficient for a hardware partner to bid against — with facility-specific verification protocols deferred to v1.2 once partner facilities are selected.

hard_fail_floors.name: Suit-attributable LOC probability
hard_fail_floors.assertion: P(crew loss attributable to suit failure | mission) <= 1.0e-3 per mission, evaluated via system-level probabilistic risk assessment.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: Artemis target for total LOC is approximately 1/75 across program. Suit subsystem must be a fractional, not dominant, contributor.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Decomposed PRA: top-level LOC tree built per NASA NPR 8705.5 methodology. Each leaf is a subsystem reliability requirement assigned to a hardware subsystem (pressure shell breach, PLSS failure, comm loss with crew incapacitation, etc.). Leaf-level reliability requirements become flow-down requirements to subsystem suppliers. HF-1 is verified at top level when leaf-level requirements are met and the tree composition computes to <= 1.0e-3.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed PRA tree document (PDF + structured tree in JSON/XML), with leaf-level reliability requirements and uncertainty bounds.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: Verification methodology revised from 'Monte Carlo PRA, 1e6 trials' (which assumed a complete reliability model exists) to 'decomposed PRA with leaf-level flow-down' (which is buildable now and decomposes the problem to procurable subsystem requirements).

hard_fail_floors.name: Multi-mode operation across microgravity and partial gravity
hard_fail_floors.assertion: Single primary pressure shell operates in both microgravity (≤ 1e-3 g) and Mars surface gravity (0.30-0.40 g) with no shell swap. Boot, glove, and thermal cassettes are the only permitted mode-swap items. All cassette swaps are achievable in <= 60 minutes per crew member with onboard tools.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: Mass and logistics budget precludes two separate suit fleets. xEMU is not microgravity-rated; EMU is not surface-rated. Convergence is unsolved.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Three-stage validation: (1) parabolic flight (~25 s μg periods) — validates short-duration mobility and stowage; (2) lunar analog facility — validates partial-g task envelope at 1/6 g, with extrapolation rationale to Mars 0.38 g; (3) ISS suit-port architecture comparison — validates microgravity stowage and donning. Honest gap: no facility provides 210 days continuous microgravity or 480 days partial-g; integrated multi-month exposure is validated by accelerated life testing of seals, bearings, and elastomers under thermal/pressure cycling, with extrapolation methodology and uncertainty bounds documented.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed validation report for each of the three stages, plus an extrapolation rationale document with stated uncertainty.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: Verification expanded from 'task battery in parabolic flight + lunar analog' to a three-stage protocol with explicit acknowledgment that pre-flight validation cannot fully match multi-year mission exposure, plus accelerated-life testing methodology.

hard_fail_floors.name: EVA cadence without resupply, anchored to mission manifest
hard_fail_floors.assertion: System supports >= 250 productive EVAs across all crew over full mission, with onboard ISRU + spares only. A 'productive' EVA is defined as >= 4 hours of useful task time AND completion of at least one task from the mission EVA manifest (per NASA DRA 5.0 surface campaign or equivalent reference architecture; reference manifest is itself committed as a v1.1 dependency).
hard_fail_floors.rationale: Reference manifest projects approximately 50 EVAs per crew over 480 surface days (200 total) with engineering margin. 250 provides ~25% headroom over the manifest. Current EMU is rated to ~25 EVAs before refurb; this is a 10x improvement.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Accelerated life test on PLSS + bearings + seals + dust mitigation under simulated Mars chamber for 250-cycle equivalent. Per-EVA acceptance criteria: a candidate EVA counts if and only if (a) duration >= 4 hours productive, (b) the task closes per the mission manifest acceptance criteria for that EVA class, and (c) post-EVA suit health check passes per defined health-check protocol.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed accelerated-life test report + manifest-closure log + post-EVA health-check trace per simulated EVA.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: Added (a) mission manifest anchoring (250 = manifest + 25% margin, not arbitrary), (b) task-closure sub-floor preventing trivial short-EVA cheat, (c) post-EVA health-check requirement.
hard_fail_floors.speculative_rationale: No accelerated-life precedent at 10x EMU baseline cadence. This floor may force a v1.2 revision in 12-24 months as accelerated-life data emerges.

hard_fail_floors.name: Pre-breathe budget
hard_fail_floors.assertion: Cabin-to-EVA-ready time <= 30 minutes at any cabin pressure between 8.2 and 14.7 psia. The 30-minute budget includes any in-suit pre-breathe protocol but excludes pre-EVA suit donning.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: Pre-breathe is the dominant constraint on EVA cadence. xEMU target is 30 min at 8.2 psia cabin / 4.3 psi suit. Generalizing across cabin pressures supports varied habitat designs.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Hyperbaric chamber DCS biomarker monitoring per NASA-STD-3001 Vol 2: ≥ 8 subjects per cabin pressure setpoint (8.2, 10.2, 12.4, 14.7 psia), with venous gas emboli monitoring via precordial Doppler, Spencer score logged, decompression sickness symptoms tracked over 24-hour post-test window. Pass requires zero Type II DCS events and Spencer score distribution within FAA/NASA reference envelope.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed IRB-approved protocol, signed test report with per-subject Spencer scores and 24-hour follow-up.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: Specified protocol per NASA-STD-3001 with explicit subject count, instrumentation, and pass criteria.

hard_fail_floors.name: Dust intrusion
hard_fail_floors.assertion: Mass of Mars regolith simulant intruding into pressure garment interior over 480 simulated sols satisfies BOTH: (a) <= 0.1% of suit dry mass AND (b) <= 50 grams absolute. Both conditions must hold.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: Apollo demonstrated severe LEM dust intrusion in 3 days. Mars surface stay is 480 days. Suit-port donning architecture plus electrostatic ejection plus sealed bearings required. The absolute floor closes the trivial-pass cheat of arbitrarily heavy shell.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: JSC Mars-1A regolith chamber, 480 sols simulated with diurnal thermal cycling (-125°C to +20°C surface, with internal suit-thermal-control active), suit-port donning/doffing cycles per mission manifest. Mass measurement via post-test pressure-garment interior swab and load-cell weighing with calibrated reference; particle size distribution via SEM on collected sample.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed chamber test report + photographic log + SEM particle analysis.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: Added absolute mass floor (50 g) to prevent ratio-trick gaming. Specified SEM analysis for particle size distribution.

hard_fail_floors.name: Cumulative radiation dose from EVA exposure
hard_fail_floors.assertion: Sum of equivalent dose attributable to suit-rated EVAs over 480 surface days <= 150 mSv, excluding solar particle event (SPE) exposure for which external shelter is assumed available. If operational SPE-shelter assumption fails (no external shelter), HF-6 escalates: suit must provide >= 10 g/cm^2 hydrogen-equivalent shielding for at least 4-hour SPE survival of an unsheltered crew member.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: NASA permissible exposure limit ranges 600-1200 mSv career; suit-EVA fraction must remain a minority of total mission dose budget. SPE shelter assumption is operationally fragile and must be conditional.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: GCR transport modeling via NASA HZETRN (or OLTARIS) through suit-as-built mass model with material properties, validated against ground accelerator beam tests on garment coupons at NASA Space Radiation Laboratory (NSRL) or equivalent. SPE-survival sub-assertion verified by deployable SPE-shelter element design + 4-hour proton beam test profile mapping to a reference SPE.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed transport modeling report + NSRL beam test reports + (conditional) SPE-shelter design and test report.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: Added conditional SPE-survival escalation clause closing the SPE-exclusion trivial-pass.

hard_fail_floors.name: Repair autonomy
hard_fail_floors.assertion: >= 80% of suit failure modes (by frequency-weighted FMEA) are repairable in <= 24 hours using only onboard ISRU manufacturing + spares manifest, with no Earth communication required. Repair definition: restoration of full or graceful-degraded operational capability. Cap on subsystem-level whole-replacement repairs at 30% of total repair count (preventing FMEA-spacing gaming).
hard_fail_floors.rationale: 22-light-minute round trip and possible blackout windows preclude Earth-loop diagnosis for time-critical failures. Field-repairability is non-negotiable. The 30% cap forces fine-grained repair capability, not coarse swap-the-module patterns.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: FMEA produced per MIL-STD-1629A with frequency weighting from accelerated-life test data. Per-mode repair procedures documented. End-to-end repair drills executed in analog environment (HERA or similar) with comm blackout enforced. Drill metric: time-to-recover from injected failure, with 80% of drill failures resolved in <= 24 hours and <= 30% of resolutions involving subsystem-level replacement.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed FMEA + repair procedures library + analog facility drill log.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: Added 30% cap on subsystem-replacement repairs (closes coarse-FMEA gaming). Specified analog facility validation.

hard_fail_floors.name: Decision provenance, append-every-attempt
hard_fail_floors.assertion: 100% of safety-critical decision EVENTS (defined as any decision attempt — autonomous resolution, gate request, gate approval, gate rejection, timeout, fallback-to-safe-passive — affecting O2, CO2, pressure, temperature, suit power, or crew biomedical alarms) produce an append-only cryptographically signed provenance record on a hash-chained ledger. Audit metric is fraction-unlogged at post-mission ledger replay, target = 0. Successful chain integrity verifiable post-mission with zero false-positive tampering detections over 480 days.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: Liability, medical, regulatory, and insurance posture for autonomous life-support require post-hoc audit. Maps to Aletheia DAC pattern. Append-every-attempt (not just successful decisions) closes the silent-failure cheat.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Red-team injection of 1,000 falsified telemetry events into PLSS bus during simulated mission; verify ledger detects injection within 1 second of event. Chain replay verification at mission end with formal verification of chain integrity. Fraction-unlogged audit: replay all simulated decision events from authoritative source-of-truth log, verify each appears in chain.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Red-team injection test report (signed) + chain integrity verification report + fraction-unlogged audit report.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: Reframed from 'signed decisions' to 'append every attempt' explicitly including failures, rejections, timeouts, fallbacks. Specified red-team injection count and detection latency.

hard_fail_floors.name: Approval gate integrity
hard_fail_floors.assertion: 100% of decisions classified as REQUIRES_HUMAN under the suit's approval-gate policy either (a) receive crew or ground approval before execution, or (b) fall back to a documented safe-passive state. The REQUIRES_HUMAN classification policy file is itself a v1.1 dependency artifact, cryptographically committed to the same chain as the benchmark. Reclassification of any action constitutes a versioned policy update and requires (i) a new committed policy version and (ii) explicit human authorization recorded on chain.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: MVCI approval-gate pattern. Loss of communication must not collapse to autonomous execution; it must collapse to a documented safe state. Policy binding prevents reclassification-out-of-REQUIRES_HUMAN cheat.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Adversarial testing under simulated 14-day blackout, including induced decision-required scenarios across all REQUIRES_HUMAN action classes. Verify no unauthorized executions in audit trail. Verify policy file hash matches chain commit at every decision event.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed adversarial test report + policy-file integrity log per decision event.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: Added policy file as cryptographically committed dependency. Reclassification rules made explicit.

hard_fail_floors.name: Safety-critical deterministic kernel, hardware-isolated
hard_fail_floors.assertion: All decisions in the safety-critical loop (O2 valve actuation, emergency pressure dump, CO2 scrubber switchover, primary battery cutoff) execute on a formally verified deterministic kernel with worst-case execution time bounded and proven, independent of any ML inference subsystem. The kernel runs on hardware-isolated compute with bus-level enforcement: the ML subsystem CANNOT bypass the kernel at the bus level, not by policy.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: ML inference is non-deterministic and not formally verifiable at current SOTA. Life-critical actions require a verifiable substrate. Mercury Subleq is a candidate substrate. Hardware isolation closes the kernel-stub-and-bypass cheat.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Formal verification artifacts (Coq/Lean/F* or equivalent) covering kernel ISA semantics, control-loop correctness, and WCET bounds. Bus-level isolation verified by hardware-architecture analysis: ML subsystem has no privileged access to safety-bus; kernel is the only writer. Watchdog separate from kernel. Penetration testing: red team attempts to actuate safety-bus from ML subsystem.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Formal verification proofs (machine-checkable) + WCET analysis + hardware-architecture review + penetration test report.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: Hardware-isolation requirement made explicit (bus-level, not policy-level). Penetration testing added.

hard_fail_floors.name: Interoperability with habitat and rover
hard_fail_floors.assertion: Suit interoperates with at least one habitat suit-port specification AND at least one rover suit-port specification. Both interface specifications are published documents committed to the chain as v1.1 dependencies. Suit-to-port mate/demate succeeds in <= 5 minutes per nominal cycle.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: The suit does not exist in isolation. Suit-port architecture is the dust-mitigation and pre-breathe enabler; interoperability is the operational requirement.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Bench-level mate/demate testing against published port specs, with mechanical, pneumatic, electrical, and data interface verification per interface control document. >= 100 mate/demate cycles per port, with no degradation of seal integrity (verified by helium leak test).
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed ICDs + cycle test report + leak test report.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: New floor added in Phase 3 (coverage attack — suit does not exist in isolation).

hard_fail_floors.name: Effective autonomy ratio on autonomous-eligible decisions
hard_fail_floors.assertion: Of decision events classified by the v1.1-committed policy file as AUTONOMOUS_ELIGIBLE, the system resolves at least 70% autonomously (not safe-passive fallback) with mission task continuity maintained, evaluated across the v1.1 simulation test suite. The 70% threshold is anchored to the design intent that AUTONOMOUS_ELIGIBLE decisions are by construction within ACI bands; if the system safe-passive-fallbacks more than 30% of those, the substrate is mis-calibrated.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: Counters the Goodhart pathological optimum (a system that passes HF-8/HF-9 by collapsing all decisions to safe-passive). The system cannot pass by abdicating. Threshold anchored to ACI band design, not arbitrary.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: v1.1 simulation test suite (committed to chain): >= 10,000 decision events drawn from a stratified scenario library covering nominal, edge, and adversarial operational conditions. Each event is tagged at scenario-design time with its policy classification. Autonomous-resolution rate measured on AUTONOMOUS_ELIGIBLE events.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed simulation test report with per-scenario classification and resolution outcome.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: New floor added in Phase 3 (Goodhart attack). Threshold revised from arbitrary 60% to design-intent-anchored 70% on AUTONOMOUS_ELIGIBLE subset.

hard_fail_floors.name: Biomedical alert latency
hard_fail_floors.assertion: End-to-end latency from biomedical sensor event detection to crew-perceptible alert <= 5 seconds, INCLUDING chain signing of the alert event. Alert classes: cardiac arrhythmia, hypoxia, hypercapnia, hyperthermia, pressure loss, sudden loss of consciousness indicator.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: Cross-investigator (flight surgeon) attack: 30+ second alert delay on arrhythmia can kill. Chain signing is part of audit posture and must fit in the budget, not bypass it.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Bench testing with simulated sensor events at known timestamps; measure end-to-end latency over >= 1,000 trials per alert class. p99 latency <= 5 s. Verify each alert event has signed chain entry within latency budget.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed latency test report with per-trial timestamps and chain-entry hashes.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: New floor added in Phase 3 (cross-investigator attack).

hard_fail_floors.name: Kernel re-verification on modification
hard_fail_floors.assertion: Any post-deployment modification to the safety-critical kernel firmware requires a signed re-verification artifact (formal verification re-run + WCET re-analysis) committed to the chain BEFORE deployment. Unverified modifications cannot deploy.
hard_fail_floors.rationale: Cross-investigator (certification engineer) attack: field updates invalidate formal verification. Configuration management is binding.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Process audit: simulate 10 modification cycles (patches, parameter updates, capability extensions). Verify each deployment is preceded by a committed re-verification artifact. Attempt one unauthorized deployment in audit; verify system rejects.
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed process audit report + deployment rejection log.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: New floor added in Phase 3 (cross-investigator attack).

hard_fail_floors.name: Embodied-AI compliance evidence package
hard_fail_floors.assertion: System produces a documented evidence package mapping all hard-fail floors to clauses in: (a) ISO 12100 (machine safety risk assessment), (b) ANSI/RIA R15.08-1 (mobile robot safety), (c) ANSI/A3 R15.06-2025 (industrial robot safety) and ANSI/A3 R15.08-2 (autonomy levels for mobile robots), (d) OMB M-25-22 (AIBOM extension for embodied AI), (e) HIPAA (biomedical telemetry on US crewed missions), (f) NASA-STD-3001 (crew health and performance).
hard_fail_floors.rationale: Cross-investigator (actuarial underwriter) attack: insurance cannot price without standards mapping. Liability, certification, and procurement all require this package. The active 90-day AIBOM plan is a prerequisite for the M-25-22 evidence component.
hard_fail_floors.verification_methodology: Document review by qualified compliance reviewer; verify each hard-fail floor has a documented clause mapping with supporting evidence reference. Underwriter dry-run: a third-party underwriter reviews the package and confirms it is sufficient to begin a policy quote process (not a policy issuance — just a quote process).
hard_fail_floors.verification_artifact: Signed evidence package + reviewer sign-off + underwriter dry-run letter.
hard_fail_floors.v1_0_to_v1_1_change: New floor added in Phase 3 (cross-investigator attack). Explicit dependency on 90-day AIBOM plan acknowledged.

soft_scoring.weight: 0-10 per criterion. Hard-fail floors must pass before soft scoring is meaningful.

criteria.name: Total system dry mass per suit, kg
Joint torque deviation from unsuited baseline, mean across major joints, Nm
Total field of view, degrees solid angle
Biomedical telemetry channels with signed provenance
Mean time to repair from FMEA failure modes, hours
Training time to certified EVA, hours
NASA TLX subjective workload across standard EVA task battery (now a soft floor with threshold = EMU baseline + 15% tolerance)
Comm bandwidth tolerance, minimum kbps for nominal operation

non_stationarity_clause.summary: All assertions evaluated under both stationary and drift conditions. Static conformal coverage tested at 1x, 2x, 3x distribution shift on five named distributions. Pass requires Adaptive Conformal Inference (ACI) coverage >= 0.85 under 3x shift.
named_distributions: Crew metabolic rate (drift across deconditioning, age, illness episodes; surface EVA exertion patterns)
Thermal load on PLSS (drift across solar irradiance, surface vs cave, season, transit solar-side vs shadow-side)
CO2 partial pressure profile (drift across crew exertion patterns and CO2 scrubber efficiency aging)
Dust load rate on filters and bearings (monotonic accumulation with rate-drift across surface activity profile)
Bearing wear rate (monotonic with rate-drift across cycle count and contamination)
non_stationarity_clause.verification_methodology: Aletheia DAC ACI evaluation harness, evaluated on synthetic and (where available) flight-analog datasets across the five distributions at 1x, 2x, 3x shift.

trivial_pass_strategies_v1_1: HF-12 + HF-9 composition: a system that reclassifies actions out of AUTONOMOUS_ELIGIBLE to evade HF-12 (sandbagging). Mitigation: AUTONOMOUS_ELIGIBLE classifications are policy-committed and any reclassification triggers versioned re-commit (per HF-9). Drift in the eligible-set distribution is itself audited.

HF-1 leaf-level gaming: assign aggressive leaf reliability to non-existent subsystems and let composition pass. Mitigation: leaf-level requirements are flow-down to procurement; supplier evidence is required at procurement-package phase, not just spec phase.
HF-2 extrapolation overreach: extrapolation rationale claims unsupported confidence. Mitigation: uncertainty bounds required and reviewed.
HF-13 chain-signing throttle: delay signing to keep per-event latency low while violating overall throughput. Mitigation: signing throughput verified separately as part of HF-8 audit.
HF-15 evidence package volume cheat: a 500-page binder with weak mapping. Mitigation: reviewer sign-off requires specific clause-to-floor mappings, not document existence.
dependency_artifacts_to_commit_with_v1_1: Mission EVA manifest (per NASA DRA 5.0 or equivalent) — anchors HF-3
REQUIRES_HUMAN / AUTONOMOUS_ELIGIBLE policy file — anchors HF-9 and HF-12
Habitat suit-port interface specification — anchors HF-11
Rover suit-port interface specification — anchors HF-11
v1.1 simulation test suite (>= 10,000 stratified decision scenarios) — anchors HF-12 and the non-stationarity clause
open_items_for_v1_2: Facility-specific verification protocols for shell-scope floors (selected by hardware partner)
Reference accelerated-life data may force HF-3 (250 EVAs) revision
SPE-shelter operational reality may force HF-6 escalation clause to primary
v1.1 simulation test suite scenario library production (~10k scenarios)
